WHITEOUT

Command Center

Multi-Event Planning
Version 1.0.7

Events

Choose an event workspace. Each event has direct-link pages for overview, live planning and rules so you can share the exact view your team needs.

Foundry

Live objective planning, roster-based legion assignments and phase-specific building priorities.

Canyon

Placeholder workspace for future Canyon roster planning, objective guidance and event-specific assignments.

Foundry Overview

Foundry planning is built around objective control, not scattered fighting. The assignment engine prioritizes high-value buildings, keeps secondary targets close to each combatant's primary objective and limits overcrowding by capping assignments per building based on priority.

Phase 1 emphasizes Boiler, Transit and both Prototypes. Phase 2 shifts attention toward Mercenary Camp, Munitions, Boiler, Imperial Foundry and Prototypes. Phase 3 keeps Imperial Foundry and Boiler at the top while maintaining Munitions, Mercenary Camp, Prototypes and nearby support buildings.

Battlefield

Select an Objective

Tap any objective on the battlefield to view its strategy, assigned team and priority.

Rules & Best Practices

  • Capture buildings before chasing fights; points and buffs compound faster than random trades.
  • Do not overload one building. Critical objectives can carry more combatants, but every available objective should have coverage first.
  • Keep each combatant's secondary and tertiary objectives near their primary objective so rotations are realistic.
  • Lower-power combatants should lean toward east-side safe-zone objectives when possible.
  • In Phase 1, secure Boiler and Transit quickly while contesting both Prototypes.
  • In Phase 2, prioritize Mercenary Camp, Munitions and Boiler while keeping pressure on Imperial Foundry and both Prototypes.
  • In Phase 3, Imperial Foundry and Boiler are the primary anchors; Munitions, Mercenary Camp, Prototypes, Workshops and Repairs support the endgame.

Canyon Overview

Canyon planning will use the shared roster and Canyon assignment field. This placeholder keeps the navigation shape ready while the event-specific rules and assignment engine are designed.

Canyon Battlefield

Canyon battlefield tools are not active yet. This page is reserved for future map views, team assignments and direct links once Canyon logic is added.

Canyon Rules & Best Practices

  • Use the roster Canyon Assignment field to stage future event-specific team planning.
  • Keep manual notes close to the roster until Canyon scoring, objectives and assignment rules are defined.
  • This page will become the direct-link reference for Canyon strategy once the module is implemented.

Roster

The roster is shared across events. Import remains the protected editing surface for now, while this view provides a read-only planning reference for combatant data used by Foundry and future event modules.

Import & Roster Editor
